##NAMES=Schindlerite
##RRUFFID=R250082
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY={(NH_4_)_4_Na_2_(H_2_O)_10_}{V_10_O_28_}
##LOCALITY=St. Jude mine, Slick Rock district, San Miguel County, Colorado, US
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Orange tabular crystals that commonly occur in stacked parallel intergrowths. Type sample, describe by Kampf et al (2013)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250082
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY={[(Na_1.58_K_0.35_Ca_0.02_Sr_0.01_)(H_2_O)_10.00_][(H_3_O)_4_]}{V_10_O_28_} ; Based on V^5+^ = 10 and O = 42
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.5143 b: 10.4283 c: 11.2827 alpha: 68.595 beta: 87.253 gamma: 67.112 volume: 854.08 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
